title: Add AUTX to x402 ecosystem (Infrastructure & Tooling)
description:
## Add AUTX to x402 ecosystem (Infrastructure & Tooling)

Adds [AUTX](https://autx.ai) to the x402 ecosystem partners list as `Infrastructure & Tooling` (same bucket as Alchemy).

**What AUTX is.** A public catalog and bonding-curve trading layer for paid AI agents on Base. We crawl `agentic.market/v1/services` (1P-vetted only), persist each as a claim-gated shadow listing, route discovery traffic via HTTP 302 to the source endpoint pre-claim (zero revenue interception), and open bonding-curve t
_raw_body:
## Add AUTX to x402 ecosystem (Infrastructure & Tooling)

Adds [AUTX](https://autx.ai) to the x402 ecosystem partners list as `Infrastructure & Tooling` (same bucket as Alchemy).

**What AUTX is.** A public catalog and bonding-curve trading layer for paid AI agents on Base. We crawl `agentic.market/v1/services` (1P-vetted only), persist each as a claim-gated shadow listing, route discovery traffic via HTTP 302 to the source endpoint pre-claim (zero revenue interception), and open bonding-curve trading only after the developer signs `AgentFactory.launchAgent()` from their own wallet. Post-claim economics: 10% platform fee, 72% to the developer in USDC, 18% buyback-and-burn on the developer's own agent token.

**Public catalog manifest** (mirrors `/v1/services` shape): https://autx.ai/x402-services.json — currently 476 services (5 native + 471 shadow).

**Relationship one-pager** (sequence diagram, regulatory posture, what AUTX will and won't do): https://autx.ai/agentic-market-one-pager

**Operational details for catalog hygiene:**
- 4-hour takedown SLA at `autx.ai/contact?takedown=<TICKER>` and a public opt-out registry at `autx.ai/opt-out.json`
- Trademark normalization (Unicode confusables, leet-speak, homoglyphs, zero-width chars) + 696-entry reserved-ticker blocklist (NYSE/NASDAQ top 500, crypto top 100, platform internals)
- Wyoming DAO LLC. Claim-gated architecture eliminates pre-claim Howey exposure.

Logo: 512×512 RGBA PNG at `typescript/site/public/logos/autx.png`.

Happy to adjust the description / category / logo if reviewers want a different fit. Thanks for the open ecosystem.

title: Add Gatefare to x402 ecosystem (Services/Endpoints)
description:
Adds [Gatefare](https://gatefare.io) to the x402 ecosystem page.

Gatefare is a non-custodial marketplace of paid HTTP APIs that uses x402 (`exact` scheme) over USDC on Base mainnet. Anyone can publish an API, get a proxy URL with a Gatefare-managed split contract, and start receiving micropayments — no escrow, no SaaS keys.

We just shipped an MCP server ([@gatefare/mcp](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@gatefare/mcp)) so agents in Claude Desktop / Cursor / any MCP-compatible client can discover a
_raw_body:
Adds [Gatefare](https://gatefare.io) to the x402 ecosystem page.

Gatefare is a non-custodial marketplace of paid HTTP APIs that uses x402 (`exact` scheme) over USDC on Base mainnet. Anyone can publish an API, get a proxy URL with a Gatefare-managed split contract, and start receiving micropayments — no escrow, no SaaS keys.

We just shipped an MCP server ([@gatefare/mcp](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@gatefare/mcp)) so agents in Claude Desktop / Cursor / any MCP-compatible client can discover and call paid APIs through `gatefare.call_api` — the server handles the full 402 → EIP-3009 sign → retry handshake automatically. We're hoping this makes it easier for agent builders to start using x402 without writing the signing logic themselves.

**Files added:**
- `typescript/site/app/ecosystem/partners-data/gatefare/metadata.json`
- `typescript/site/public/logos/gatefare.png`

**Category:** Services/Endpoints (we're an x402 service consumer + endpoint marketplace).

Happy to iterate on the description / category if a different framing fits better.

- Site: https://gatefare.io
- MCP: https://github.com/gatefareio/mcp-server
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@gatefare/mcp

title: test(python/evm): add unit tests for eip712 and erc6492 helpers
description:
## Summary

Add **55 unit tests** across two EVM mechanism source files that previously had **zero unit test coverage**: `eip712.py` and `erc6492.py`.

## `eip712.py` — 34 tests

| Function | Tests |
|---|---|
| `_encode_type` | known type, TransferWithAuthorization, unknown → empty, single-field custom |
| `_type_hash` | 32-byte output, deterministic, differs across types |
| `_encode_data` | bytes output, unknown type raises, missing field raises, unsupported raises, bytes-as-hex, bool, int |
_raw_body:
## Summary

Add **55 unit tests** across two EVM mechanism source files that previously had **zero unit test coverage**: `eip712.py` and `erc6492.py`.

## `eip712.py` — 34 tests

| Function | Tests |
|---|---|
| `_encode_type` | known type, TransferWithAuthorization, unknown → empty, single-field custom |
| `_type_hash` | 32-byte output, deterministic, differs across types |
| `_encode_data` | bytes output, unknown type raises, missing field raises, unsupported raises, bytes-as-hex, bool, int |
| `hash_struct` | 32-byte output, deterministic, different data differs |
| `hash_domain` | 32-byte output, deterministic, differs by chain / name / contract |
| `hash_typed_data` | 32-byte output, deterministic, differs by domain / amount |
| `hash_eip3009_authorization` | 32-byte output, deterministic, differs by amount / chain / token name |
| `build_typed_data_for_signing` | 4-tuple, primary_type, domain chain_id, message fields present, hash == `hash_eip3009_authorization` |

## `erc6492.py` — 21 tests

| Function | Tests |
|---|---|
| `is_erc6492_signature` | <32 bytes, empty, 65-byte EOA, magic suffix, magic alone, wrong suffix |
| `parse_erc6492_signature` | non-ERC-6492 passthrough, valid ABI decode, invalid ABI → ValueError, no-raise for plain sig |
| `is_eoa_signature` | 65-byte zero-factory, non-65-byte, non-zero factory, empty inner |
| `has_deployment_info` | non-zero factory + calldata, zero factory, non-zero factory empty calldata, zero factory with calldata |

## Verification

```
55 passed, 1 warning in 0.17s
```

All tests run in <0.2s with zero external network calls.

_raw_body:
## Summary

Add 71 unit tests for `x402.schemas.helpers` — the only module in the `schemas` package without a dedicated unit test file.

## What's tested

9 test classes covering all 10 public helpers:

| Helper | Tests | What's covered |
|---|---|---|
| `detect_version` | 9 | bytes/dict inputs, versions 1 & 2, missing field, null, invalid 0/3 |
| `get_scheme_and_network` | 9 | v1 top-level fields, v2 `accepted` block, bytes, each missing-field error |
| `match_payload_to_requirements` | 9 | v1 scheme+network match/mismatch, v2 five-field check, bytes round-trip |
| `parse_payment_required` | 8 | v1→`PaymentRequiredV1`, v2→`PaymentRequired`, bytes, field preservation, error |
| `parse_payment_payload` | 6 | v1→`PaymentPayloadV1`, v2→`PaymentPayload`, bytes, payload preservation |
| `parse_payment_requirements` | 6 | v1/v2 dispatch, bytes, invalid version 0 and 3 |
| `matches_network_pattern` | 9 | exact, wildcard, cross-namespace rejection, bare-namespace edge case |
| `derive_network_pattern` | 7 | single/many same-namespace→wildcard, mixed→first, empty list |
| `find_schemes_by_network` | 8 | exact-before-wildcard priority, wildcard fallback, miss, empty map |

## Stats

- **~0.04 s** total runtime (zero network calls, no blockchain state)

## Summary

title: Add Voidly Pay (Facilitator) to ecosystem directory
description:
Adding Voidly Pay to `typescript/site/app/ecosystem/partners-data/` per the README contribution flow.

**What it is**

Voidly Pay is an x402 facilitator + agent-to-agent payment rail. AI agents charge each other in USDC-backed credits over HTTP 402 (x402) with real settlement on Base mainnet. Discovery endpoint:

- https://api.voidly.ai/v1/pay/x402/discovery — returns the canonical x402 schema (facilitator URL, schemes, endpoints).
- https://api.voidly.ai/v1/pay/manifest.json — full machine-read
_raw_body:
Adding Voidly Pay to `typescript/site/app/ecosystem/partners-data/` per the README contribution flow.

**What it is**

Voidly Pay is an x402 facilitator + agent-to-agent payment rail. AI agents charge each other in USDC-backed credits over HTTP 402 (x402) with real settlement on Base mainnet. Discovery endpoint:

- https://api.voidly.ai/v1/pay/x402/discovery — returns the canonical x402 schema (facilitator URL, schemes, endpoints).
- https://api.voidly.ai/v1/pay/manifest.json — full machine-readable surface.

**Beyond the facilitator** (additive, not on the metadata.json above): a 28-tool MCP server (`@voidly/pay-mcp`), drop-in SDKs for TypeScript / Python / LangChain / Vercel AI / FastAPI / Hono / Express / Flask, a CLI (`@voidly/pay-cli`), a one-command scaffolder (`npx create-voidly-agent`), a public reserves dashboard (vault USDC ≥ credits, refreshed every 15s), and a universal reverse proxy that paywalls any public HTTPS URL with one query parameter — no SDK install required by the URL owner.

**Trust posture**

- Stage 2 vault on Base mainnet at \`0xb592512932a7b354969bb48039c2dc7ad6ad1c12\`, source-verified on Sourcify ([full_match](https://repo.sourcify.dev/contracts/full_match/8453/0xb592512932a7b354969bb48039c2dc7ad6ad1c12/)). Non-upgradable. EIP-1153 transient-storage reentrancy guard.
- Daily cap $100k, per-tx cap $1k (governance-tunable).
- Proof of reserves: https://voidly.ai/pay/proof
- Live no-install demo (Hugging Face Space): https://huggingface.co/spaces/emperor-mew/voidly-pay

**This PR**

- Adds \`typescript/site/app/ecosystem/partners-data/voidly-pay/metadata.json\`
- Adds \`typescript/site/public/logos/voidly-pay.png\` (400×400 PNG)
- Category: Facilitators (we're a facilitator that supports verify+settle on Base)

Happy to add follow-up entries under Client-Side Integrations (the SDK family) or Services/Endpoints (the universal proxy + the paid endpoints we run) if you'd prefer those split out — let me know.

Repo: https://github.com/voidly-ai/voidly-pay
Discovery: https://api.voidly.ai/v1/pay/x402/discovery
Docs: https://voidly.ai/pay

title: test(ts/core): add unit tests for findFacilitatorBySchemeAndNetwork and Base64EncodedRegex
description:

### `findFacilitatorBySchemeAndNetwork` (8 tests)
- Exact match: scheme + network in `networks` Set → returns facilitator
- Missing scheme → returns `undefined`  
- Network not in Set and doesn't match pattern → returns `undefined`
- Pattern fallback: network not in Set but matches `pattern` wildcard → returns facilitator
- Empty map → returns `undefined`

_raw_body:
## Summary

Adds 18 unit tests covering two exports from `@x402/core` utils that had **zero prior test coverage**:

### `findFacilitatorBySchemeAndNetwork` (8 tests)
- Exact match: scheme + network in `networks` Set → returns facilitator
- Missing scheme → returns `undefined`  
- Network not in Set and doesn't match pattern → returns `undefined`
- Pattern fallback: network not in Set but matches `pattern` wildcard → returns facilitator
- Empty map → returns `undefined`
- Multi-scheme map: returns correct facilitator per scheme
- Set match takes priority over pattern (coverage of the early-return path)
- Non-string facilitator (object) returns correctly

### `Base64EncodedRegex` (10 tests)
- Valid base64: no padding, single `=`, double `==`
- Empty string (valid)
- Full alphabet (`A-Za-z0-9+/`)
- Realistic JWT segment
- Rejects: hyphen, underscore (base64url), spaces, triple `===` padding

## Changes
- `typescript/packages/core/test/unit/utils/utils.test.ts` — 122 lines added (+24 tests, 74 total from 50)
- `typescript/.changeset/test-ts-core-utils-facilitator-regex-coverage.md` — changeset fragment

All 74 tests in the file pass. No production code changes.

title: test(ts/core): add unit tests for x402HTTPClient codec methods
description:
## Summary

Add 18 unit tests for the three codec/transport methods on `x402HTTPClient` that had no prior test coverage. The existing `x402HTTPClient.hooks.test.ts` only covered the `onPaymentRequired`/`handlePaymentRequired` lifecycle hooks — the codec methods were exercised only indirectly through integration tests.

## New test file: `x402HTTPClient.codec.test.ts`

### `encodePaymentSignatureHeader`
- Returns `PAYMENT-SIGNATURE` header for v2 payload (with base64 round-trip assertion)
- Retur
_raw_body:
## Summary

Add 18 unit tests for the three codec/transport methods on `x402HTTPClient` that had no prior test coverage. The existing `x402HTTPClient.hooks.test.ts` only covered the `onPaymentRequired`/`handlePaymentRequired` lifecycle hooks — the codec methods were exercised only indirectly through integration tests.

## New test file: `x402HTTPClient.codec.test.ts`

### `encodePaymentSignatureHeader`
- Returns `PAYMENT-SIGNATURE` header for v2 payload (with base64 round-trip assertion)
- Returns `X-PAYMENT` header for v1 payload (with base64 round-trip assertion)
- Throws `"Unsupported x402 version"` for an unrecognised version
- v1 and v2 use distinct header keys

### `getPaymentRequiredResponse`
- Reads `PAYMENT-REQUIRED` header for v2
- Falls back to body when `x402Version === 1` and header is absent
- Prefers v2 header over v1 body when both are present
- Throws `"Invalid payment required response"` when neither header nor valid body is present
- Throws when body lacks `x402Version` field
- Throws when body `x402Version` is not `1` (e.g. `2` without a header)
- Treats `undefined` `getHeader` return value as absent

### `getPaymentSettleResponse`
- Reads `PAYMENT-RESPONSE` header for v2
- Falls back to `X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE` for v1
- Prefers `PAYMENT-RESPONSE` over `X-PAYMENT-RESPONSE` when both are present
- Throws `"Payment response header not found"` when no payment response header is present
- Treats `undefined` `getHeader` return value as absent
- Returned `SettleResponse` preserves all fields (`success`, `transaction`, `network`)

### `createPaymentPayload` (delegation smoke test)
- Confirms delegation to the underlying `x402Client` by verifying it throws without a signer (the expected behaviour from the client layer)

## Testing
```
Test Files  17 passed (17)
     Tests  404 passed (404)  (+18 new)
```

## Changeset
`typescript/.changeset/test-x402http-client-codec-coverage.md` — `@x402/core` patch

_raw_body:
## Summary

Fills the remaining gaps in `extensionResponsePolicy.test.ts`, growing the suite from **11 → 31 tests** with zero new test infrastructure.

## What was missing

### `snapshotPaymentRequirementsList` (untested directly)
- extra deep-clone isolation — mutating the original after snapshotting must not change the snapshot
- empty list returns `[]`
- multi-entry order and field preservation

### `snapshotSettleResponseCore` (untested directly)
- all seven core fields captured correctly
- `extensions` field is **not** included in the snapshot

### `assertAcceptsAllowlistedAfterExtensionEnrich` (6 new cases)
- accepts-length **increase** → throws
- accepts-length **decrease** → throws
- `network` mutation → throws (scheme/network branch)
- `maxTimeoutSeconds` mutation → throws
- non-vacant `payTo` mutation → throws
- non-vacant `asset` mutation → throws
- empty arrays → no-op pass
- extension key appears in error messages

### `assertSettleResponseCoreUnchanged` (6 new cases)
- `success` change → throws
- `network` change → throws
- `amount` change → throws
- `payer` change → throws
- `errorReason` change → throws
- `errorMessage` change → throws
- extension key appears in error messages

## Test results

```
Test Files  17 passed (17)
     Tests  437 passed (437)
```

All 437 existing tests continue to pass.

title: test(ts/core): add unit tests for HTTPFacilitatorClient 429 retry, auth headers, and error truncation
description:
## Summary

Adds 17 unit tests to `httpFacilitatorClient.test.ts` covering three previously untested code paths in `HTTPFacilitatorClient`:

### 429 retry with exponential backoff (`getSupported`)
- Retries on 429 and succeeds on 2nd attempt
- Retries on 429 and succeeds on 3rd (final) attempt
- Throws after exhausting all 3 retries
- Does **not** retry on non-429 errors (e.g. 500)

### `createAuthHeaders()`
- Returns empty headers when no `createAuthHeaders` config is provided
- Returns per-pat
_raw_body:
## Summary

Adds 17 unit tests to `httpFacilitatorClient.test.ts` covering three previously untested code paths in `HTTPFacilitatorClient`:

### 429 retry with exponential backoff (`getSupported`)
- Retries on 429 and succeeds on 2nd attempt
- Retries on 429 and succeeds on 3rd (final) attempt
- Throws after exhausting all 3 retries
- Does **not** retry on non-429 errors (e.g. 500)

### `createAuthHeaders()`
- Returns empty headers when no `createAuthHeaders` config is provided
- Returns per-path headers when config is provided
- Returns empty headers for unknown paths (graceful fallback)

### Auth header propagation
- Auth headers from `createAuthHeaders` config are forwarded in `verify()` requests
- Auth headers from `createAuthHeaders` config are forwarded in `settle()` requests
- Auth headers from `createAuthHeaders` config are forwarded in `getSupported()` requests

### Error message truncation
- Long response bodies (>200 chars) are truncated with ellipsis in error messages
- Short response bodies are included verbatim

## Testing
All 17 new tests pass alongside the existing 411 tests (428 total, no regressions).

> AI-assisted: Tests were generated with Claude Code and reviewed for correctness.

title: feat(python): add on_payment_required hook to x402HTTPClient
description:
## Summary

Implements `on_payment_required` lifecycle hooks for Python's `x402HTTPClient` and `x402HTTPClientSync`, matching the existing TypeScript `x402HTTPClient.onPaymentRequired` API that was previously missing from Python (marked *"Coming soon"* in the docs).

## What Changed

**`python/x402/http/x402_http_client.py`**
- Added `on_payment_required(hook)` and `handle_payment_required(payment_required)` to both `x402HTTPClient` (async) and `x402HTTPClientSync`
- Hook receives the parsed `Pa
_raw_body:
## Summary

Implements `on_payment_required` lifecycle hooks for Python's `x402HTTPClient` and `x402HTTPClientSync`, matching the existing TypeScript `x402HTTPClient.onPaymentRequired` API that was previously missing from Python (marked *"Coming soon"* in the docs).

## What Changed

**`python/x402/http/x402_http_client.py`**
- Added `on_payment_required(hook)` and `handle_payment_required(payment_required)` to both `x402HTTPClient` (async) and `x402HTTPClientSync`
- Hook receives the parsed `PaymentRequired` object; returning `dict[str, str]` retries with those headers (skipping payment), returning `None` falls through to normal payment flow
- First hook to return non-`None` wins; subsequent hooks are skipped
- Method chaining supported on `on_payment_required()`
- `handle_402_response` now runs hooks before creating a payment payload; returns `(headers, None)` when a hook short-circuits

**`python/x402/tests/unit/http/test_x402_http_client.py`**
- 16 new unit tests covering: no-hook fall-through, hook short-circuit, hook ordering (first wins), second-hook fallback, chaining, and integration with `handle_402_response`

**`docs/advanced-concepts/lifecycle-hooks.mdx`**
- Replaced *Coming soon* placeholder with working Python sync and async examples

**`python/x402/changelog.d/120.feature.md`**
- Towncrier changelog fragment

## Testing

```
827 passed, 1 warning in 1.10s
```

All existing tests continue to pass. 16 new tests added.

## Notes

AI-assisted contribution (0xAxiom / OpenClaw). Output reviewed for correctness against the TypeScript reference implementation and existing Python patterns.

### Coverage added

**`simulateEip3009Transfer`** (4 tests)
- Returns `true` when `readContract` resolves — ECDSA path (65-byte sig, viem `parseSignature`)
- Returns `false` when `readContract` rejects — ECDSA path
- Takes the raw-bytes path (non-65-byte sig) and passes sig directly to the contract call

_raw_body:
## Summary

Adds 20 unit tests for the two main async helpers in `typescript/packages/mechanisms/evm/src/exact/facilitator/eip3009-utils.ts` that previously had no direct test coverage.

### Coverage added

**`simulateEip3009Transfer`** (4 tests)
- Returns `true` when `readContract` resolves — ECDSA path (65-byte sig, viem `parseSignature`)
- Returns `false` when `readContract` rejects — ECDSA path
- Takes the raw-bytes path (non-65-byte sig) and passes sig directly to the contract call
- Returns `false` on non-ECDSA path when `readContract` rejects

**`diagnoseEip3009SimulationFailure`** (16 tests)
- Returns `isValid: true` when all four multicall slots succeed and values are correct
- `ErrInsufficientBalance` when balance < amount required
- `ErrEip3009NotSupported` when `authorizationState` call fails
- `ErrEip3009NonceAlreadyUsed` when nonce is already used
- `ErrEip3009TokenNameMismatch` when token name differs from requirements
- `ErrEip3009TokenVersionMismatch` when version differs from requirements
- `ErrUnknown` when multicall itself throws
- Passes through a custom `isValid: true` response when all checks pass
- Correctly handles missing `name` / `version` requirement fields (no mismatch error)
- Verifies priority ordering: authorizationState failure > nonce > name/version > balance

### Checklist
- [x] GPG-signed commit
- [x] Changeset fragment (`@x402/evm patch`)
- [x] All 357 existing tests still pass (`pnpm --filter @x402/evm run test`)

## What is CRYPTYX?

Institutional-grade digital asset intelligence engine — 376 metrics across 8 factor classes, ~200 tracked assets. 49 x402-priced endpoints across 4 tiers ($0.01–$0.25 USDC), live on Base mainnet via CDP facilitator.

- **Pricing manifest:** https://www.cryptyx.ai/.well-known/x402-manifest.json
- **x402 docs:** https://cryptyx.ai/docs/x402

### Settled Transactions (Base Mainnet)

4 demo transactions settled on-chain (Apr 26, 2026). Wallet: `0xb0cb1E862437B98df874e4095A67D423E7d656ec`. Total: $0.17 USDC.

title: test(ts/evm): add unit tests for multicall utility
description:
## Summary

The `multicall.ts` module in `@x402/evm` is a critical batching utility used throughout the EVM facilitator (EIP-3009 simulation, diagnostic checks, Permit2 flows), but had zero unit test coverage. This PR adds 14 unit tests covering all branching paths.

## Tests added

`typescript/packages/mechanisms/evm/test/unit/multicall.test.ts`

| Case | Description |
|---|---|
| `MULTICALL3_ADDRESS` | Canonical `0xcA11bde05977b3631167028862bE2a173976CA11` value |
| Empty calls | Returns `[]`
_raw_body:
## Summary

The `multicall.ts` module in `@x402/evm` is a critical batching utility used throughout the EVM facilitator (EIP-3009 simulation, diagnostic checks, Permit2 flows), but had zero unit test coverage. This PR adds 14 unit tests covering all branching paths.

## Tests added

`typescript/packages/mechanisms/evm/test/unit/multicall.test.ts`

| Case | Description |
|---|---|
| `MULTICALL3_ADDRESS` | Canonical `0xcA11bde05977b3631167028862bE2a173976CA11` value |
| Empty calls | Returns `[]` immediately |
| Multicall3 routing | Calls `readContract` with the Multicall3 address |
| `requireSuccess=false` | tryAggregate first arg is always `false` |
| ContractCall success | ABI-decoded result returned as `{ status: 'success', result }` |
| ContractCall revert | `success: false` → `{ status: 'failure', error }` with message |
| Revert error content | `returnData` hex included in error message |
| RawContractCall success | `result` is `undefined` (no ABI available to decode) |
| RawContractCall revert | `{ status: 'failure' }` |
| Mixed array | ContractCall + RawContractCall interleaved, correct per-entry result |
| Malformed returnData | ABI decode throws → wrapped as `{ status: 'failure' }` |
| callData encoding | Encoded target address and callData forwarded correctly |
| Raw callData passthrough | `callData` field is passed verbatim without re-encoding |
| Result ordering | Three parallel calls return results in input order |

## Checklist

- [x] All 14 tests pass (`pnpm --filter @x402/evm test`)
- [x] Lint clean (`pnpm --filter @x402/evm lint`)
- [x] GPG-signed commit
- [x] Changeset fragment included

_raw_body:
## Summary

Add 24 unit tests for pure helper functions in `x402/mechanisms/svm/utils.py` that previously had **no direct test coverage**.

The existing `test_index.py` for SVM covers the functions re-exported at the package level (`normalize_network`, `validate_svm_address`, `get_usdc_address`, `convert_to_token_amount`). This PR adds a dedicated `test_utils.py` for the utility functions that are called internally or by integrators but were untested:

| Class | Functions covered |
|---|---|
| `TestGetNetworkConfig` | mainnet / devnet / testnet configs, V1 legacy names, unsupported networks |
| `TestGetAssetInfo` | default asset lookup, explicit address match, devnet, unregistered address error, unsupported network |
| `TestParseAmount` | 6-decimal USDC, whole numbers, zero, varying precisions, sub-unit truncation |
| `TestFormatAmount` | smallest-unit → string, zero, varying precisions, parse↔format round-trip |
| `TestParseMoneyToDecimal` | dollar-prefixed, plain strings, numeric types, USD/USDC suffix stripping, invalid input |

## Test results

```
uv run pytest tests/unit/mechanisms/svm/test_utils.py -v
24 passed in 0.07s

uv run pytest tests/unit/
843 passed, 1 warning in 1.05s
```

## Checklist
- [x] Tests are pure (no network I/O, no blockchain dependencies)
- [x] All existing unit tests still pass (843/843)
- [x] GPG-signed commit
- [x] Changeset fragment added

title: Add Solana Trading Data API to ecosystem
description:
## Add Solana Trading Data API

Adds a new **Services/Endpoints** entry to the x402 ecosystem.

**What it does:**
- Real-time Solana memecoin trading data for AI agents
- 3 endpoints: trending tokens, token analysis, momentum signals
- Data sourced from GeckoTerminal
- 0.01 USDC per request via x402 on Solana mainnet

**Endpoints:**
- `GET /api/trending` — Top 20 trending Solana tokens
- `GET /api/token/:mint` — Detailed analysis for a specific token
- `GET /api/momentum` — Current momentum sign
_raw_body:
## Add Solana Trading Data API

Adds a new **Services/Endpoints** entry to the x402 ecosystem.

**What it does:**
- Real-time Solana memecoin trading data for AI agents
- 3 endpoints: trending tokens, token analysis, momentum signals
- Data sourced from GeckoTerminal
- 0.01 USDC per request via x402 on Solana mainnet

**Endpoints:**
- `GET /api/trending` — Top 20 trending Solana tokens
- `GET /api/token/:mint` — Detailed analysis for a specific token
- `GET /api/momentum` — Current momentum signals and breakout detection

**Discovery:**
- `/.well-known/x402` — x402 payment discovery
- `/.well-known/agent-card.json` — A2A agent card

**Note:** Logo file not included yet — happy to add one if the team has format/size preferences.

title: fix(java): correct version in README Maven dependency snippet
description:
## Summary

The Java README installation example referenced `0.1.0-SNAPSHOT` but `java/pom.xml` is already at `1.0.0-SNAPSHOT`. This one-line fix aligns the snippet with the actual artifact version so new users copy the correct dependency.

### Change

```diff
- <version>0.1.0-SNAPSHOT</version>
+ <version>1.0.0-SNAPSHOT</version>
```

Fixes #2059 (reported by @Nihal4777)

> AI-assisted contribution (OpenClaw / Claude). Single-line doc fix, verified against pom.xml.

_raw_body:
## Summary

`parseEip3009TransferError` in `typescript/packages/mechanisms/evm/src/exact/facilitator/eip3009-utils.ts` had no test coverage. This adds 19 unit tests covering all six pattern branches:

- authorization expired / valid before → `ErrValidBeforeExpired`
- authorization not yet valid → `ErrValidAfterInFuture`
- nonce already used (three revert variants) → `ErrEip3009NonceAlreadyUsed`
- insufficient balance (three variants) → `ErrEip3009InsufficientBalance`
- invalid signature (four revert variants) → `ErrInvalidSignature`
- unknown errors / non-Error objects → `ErrTransactionFailed` fallback

All tests pass (`360 tests, 18 files`).

> AI-assisted contribution (OpenClaw / Claude). Reviewed for correctness — assertions derived from the regex patterns in the source, not fabricated.

_raw_body:
## Summary

Add 22 unit tests for exported utility functions in `typescript/packages/mechanisms/aptos/src/utils.ts` that previously had no dedicated test file. The Aptos mechanism had test coverage for `constants`, `types`, `signer`, and `index`, but `utils.ts` (4 exported functions) was untested.

## Functions covered

### `encodeAptosPayload` (8 tests)
- Encodes transaction and authenticator bytes to a valid base64 string
- Round-trips through `JSON.parse` to recover original byte arrays
- Handles empty byte arrays
- Produces deterministic output for same inputs
- Produces distinct output when transaction bytes differ
- Produces distinct output when authenticator bytes differ
- Handles large (256-byte) arrays
- Preserves boundary byte values 0 and 255

### `isEntryFunctionPayload` (7 tests)
- Returns `true` for object with `entryFunction` key (non-null value)
- Returns `false` for empty object, script payload, multisig payload
- Returns `true` when `entryFunction` is `null` or `undefined` (key presence check using `in`)
- Returns `false` for objects with other keys (e.g. `{ entry: "function" }`)

### `createAptosClient` (7 tests)
- Creates an `Aptos` client instance for mainnet (`aptos:1`)
- Creates an `Aptos` client instance for testnet (`aptos:2`)
- Accepts a custom RPC URL without error
- Returns a new instance per call (no singleton)
- Throws `"Unsupported Aptos network"` for unsupported chain IDs (`aptos:99`)
- Throws for non-Aptos namespace (`eip155:1`)
- Throws for empty string

## Test run

```
Test Files  5 passed (5)
     Tests  60 passed (60)   ← 38 existing + 22 new
```

## Changeset

Patch for `@x402/aptos` — test coverage only, no API changes.

title: fix(core/utils): harden convertToTokenAmount regex and wildcard matchers
description:
## Summary

Fixes two security hardening issues surfaced by CodeQL in CI.

### fix 1 — `convertToTokenAmount` ReDoS ([foundation#2090](https://github.com/x402-foundation/x402/issues/2090))

The input-validation regex `/^-?\d+\.?\d*$/` has overlapping quantified digit groups (`\d+` and `\d*`) around an optional decimal point. CodeQL flagged this as polynomial-time backtracking on long non-matching strings.

Replace with the non-ambiguous form:
```
/^-?(?:\d+(?:\.\d*)?)$/
```
The integer part (`\d
_raw_body:
## Summary

Fixes two security hardening issues surfaced by CodeQL in CI.

### fix 1 — `convertToTokenAmount` ReDoS ([foundation#2090](https://github.com/x402-foundation/x402/issues/2090))

The input-validation regex `/^-?\d+\.?\d*$/` has overlapping quantified digit groups (`\d+` and `\d*`) around an optional decimal point. CodeQL flagged this as polynomial-time backtracking on long non-matching strings.

Replace with the non-ambiguous form:
```
/^-?(?:\d+(?:\.\d*)?)$/
```
The integer part (`\d+`) is required; the fractional part (`(?:\.\d*)?`) is an optional non-capturing group — no overlap, no ambiguity.

Existing behaviour preserved: integers, decimals, negative values all accepted; empty/non-numeric strings and scientific notation still rejected. New adversarial tests confirm sub-100 ms rejection of 1000-digit strings with non-digit suffix.

### fix 2 — wildcard pattern matchers ([foundation#2091](https://github.com/x402-foundation/x402/issues/2091))

Three pattern-to-regex conversions used `String.prototype.replace("*", ".*")` — which replaces only the **first** `*` — and did not escape regex metacharacters, allowing literal dots or parens in stored patterns to act as regex wildcards.

Fixed in `utils/index.ts` (`findFacilitatorBySchemeAndNetwork`) and both `verify`/`settle` paths in `x402Facilitator.ts`:

```ts
// before
new RegExp("^" + schemeData.pattern.replace("*", ".*") + "$")

// after
const escapedPattern = schemeData.pattern.replace(/[+.?^{|}[\]\\]/g, "\\\$&");
new RegExp("^" + escapedPattern.replace(/\*/g, ".*") + "$")
```

New tests cover: metacharacter-in-pattern (dot treated as literal), multi-wildcard (`*:*`), cross-namespace non-match.

## Test results

All 395 existing unit tests pass. 14 new tests added covering ReDoS adversarial inputs and wildcard matcher edge cases.

## Checklist
- [x] Changeset added (`typescript/.changeset/fix-redos-convertToTokenAmount-wildcard-matchers.md`)
- [x] `pnpm lint` passes for `@x402/core`
- [x] `pnpm test` passes (395/395 tests)
- [x] GPG-signed commit

_raw_body:
## Summary

Adds 27 unit tests covering the scheme-level methods on both `ExactEvmScheme` and `UptoEvmScheme` facilitators — the last two major facilitator structs with zero direct scheme-method test coverage.

### ExactEvmScheme (`go/mechanisms/evm/exact/facilitator/scheme_test.go`) — 11 tests

| Test | Coverage |
|------|---------|
| `TestNewExactEvmScheme_NilConfig` | nil config → zero-value defaults |
| `TestNewExactEvmScheme_NonNilConfig` | non-nil config → values applied |
| `TestNewExactEvmScheme_ConfigIsCopied` | mutating caller's config struct doesn't affect scheme |
| `TestExactEvmScheme_Scheme` | returns `"exact"` |
| `TestExactEvmScheme_CaipFamily` | returns `"eip155:*"` |
| `TestExactEvmScheme_GetExtra_ReturnsNil` | always nil (no extra data for exact scheme) |
| `TestExactEvmScheme_GetSigners_ReturnsSignerAddresses` | forwards signer addresses |
| `TestExactEvmScheme_GetSigners_EmptyWhenNoAddresses` | empty slice when signer has none |
| `TestExactEvmScheme_Verify_MalformedPermit2Payload` | malformed Permit2 payload → `VerifyError(ErrInvalidPayload)` |
| `TestExactEvmScheme_Verify_NonPermit2RoutesToEIP3009_FailsOnBadNetwork` | non-Permit2 routes to EIP-3009 path; fails on unknown network |
| `TestExactEvmScheme_Settle_MalformedPermit2Payload` | malformed Permit2 payload → `SettleError(ErrInvalidPayload)` |
| `TestExactEvmScheme_Settle_NonPermit2RoutesToEIP3009_FailsOnBadNetwork` | non-Permit2 routes to EIP-3009 path; fails on unknown network |

### UptoEvmScheme (`go/mechanisms/evm/upto/facilitator/scheme_test.go`) — 16 tests

| Test | Coverage |
|------|---------|
| `TestNewUptoEvmScheme_NilConfig` | nil config → zero-value defaults |
| `TestNewUptoEvmScheme_NonNilConfig` | non-nil config → values applied |
| `TestNewUptoEvmScheme_ConfigIsCopied` | mutating caller's config struct doesn't affect scheme |
| `TestUptoEvmScheme_GetExtra_NoAddresses_ReturnsNil` | nil addresses → nil extra |
| `TestUptoEvmScheme_GetExtra_EmptyAddresses_ReturnsNil` | empty addresses → nil extra |
| `TestUptoEvmScheme_GetExtra_WithAddresses_ReturnsFacilitatorAddress` | single address → correct map key |
| `TestUptoEvmScheme_GetExtra_MultipleAddresses_ReturnsOneOfThem` | 100 iterations confirm random selection from pool |
| `TestUptoEvmScheme_GetSigners_ReturnsAddresses` | forwards signer addresses |
| `TestUptoEvmScheme_GetSigners_Empty` | empty when signer has no addresses |
| `TestUptoEvmScheme_Verify_EmptyPayload_Rejected` | empty payload → `VerifyError(ErrUptoInvalidPayload)` |
| `TestUptoEvmScheme_Verify_ExactPermit2Payload_Rejected` | exact Permit2 (no witness.facilitator) rejected |
| `TestUptoEvmScheme_Verify_NilPayloadMap_Rejected` | nil payload map → `VerifyError(ErrUptoInvalidPayload)` |
| `TestUptoEvmScheme_Settle_EmptyPayload_Rejected` | empty payload → `SettleError(ErrUptoInvalidPayload)` |
| `TestUptoEvmScheme_Settle_NilPayloadMap_Rejected` | nil payload map → `SettleError(ErrUptoInvalidPayload)` |
| `TestUptoEvmScheme_Settle_ExactPermit2Payload_Rejected` | exact Permit2 (no witness.facilitator) → `SettleError(ErrUptoInvalidPayload)` |

## Test approach

- All tests use a minimal `mockFacilitatorSigner` stub (zero network calls)
- Error assertions use `errors.As` with typed `*x402.VerifyError` / `*x402.SettleError`
- GPG-signed commit, changeset fragment included

## Checklist

- [x] 27 new unit tests all pass
- [x] `go build ./...` clean
- [x] `go vet ./...` clean
- [x] GPG-signed commit
- [x] Changeset fragment added

## Summary

Adds `permit2_helpers_test.go` with 10 unit tests covering three helpers in `go/mechanisms/evm/upto/facilitator` that previously had **zero dedicated test coverage**:

### TestAsBigInt_* (5 tests)

- nil → nil
- `int64` (not `big.Int`) → nil

### TestErrParse_* (2 tests)

- all six call-site field names (`permitted amount`, `nonce`, `deadline`, `validAfter`, `eip2612 amount`, `eip2612 deadline`)

### TestBuildUptoPermit2SettleArgs_* additional cases (3 tests)
- `ZeroValues`: all-zero decimal strings accepted without error
- `MaxUint256Amount`: full 2^256-1 parsed correctly in both `permitted.Amount` and `settlementAmount`
- `FacilitatorAddressForwarded`: `witness.Facilitator` is non-zero, confirming the upto-specific field (absent in the exact scheme) is correctly forwarded into `args.Witness.Facilitator`

## Tests added (`scheme_test.go`, 44 tests)

| Area | Tests |
|------|-------|

_raw_body:
## Summary

Adds comprehensive unit tests for `go/mechanisms/evm/upto/server/scheme.go` — a file that previously had zero test coverage.

## Tests added (`scheme_test.go`, 44 tests)

| Area | Tests |
|------|-------|
| Constructor | `NewUptoEvmScheme` returns non-nil, initialises with empty parsers |
| `Scheme()` | Returns `evm.SchemeUpto` constant |
| `GetAssetDecimals` | Known asset (6), unknown valid address (18 — Unknown Token fallback), symbol on unknown network (6 — error fallback) |
| `RegisterMoneyParser` | Returns `self` for chaining, accumulates multiple parsers |
| `ParsePrice` (map path) | amount+asset success, amount+asset+extra, non-string amount error, missing asset error, empty asset error |
| `ParsePrice` (scalar path) | `"$1.00"`, `"0.50"`, `float64`, `int`, `int64`, unsupported type (`bool`), non-numeric string, unknown network |
| `ParsePrice` (custom parser) | Custom parser takes priority; parser returning error is skipped; parser returning nil is skipped |
| `defaultMoneyConversion` metadata | Sets `assetTransferMethod=permit2`, `name`, `version` in Extra |
| `ParsePrice` (large integer) | Exercises fast integer formatting path when `amount >= oneUnit` |
| `ValidatePaymentRequirements` | Valid input, invalid payTo, empty/zero/negative/non-numeric amount, empty asset (valid), non-address asset on unknown network |

## Testing

```
go test ./mechanisms/evm/upto/server/... -count=1 -v
ok  github.com/x402-foundation/x402/go/mechanisms/evm/upto/server  (44 tests pass)
```

All tests use `eip155:84532` (Base Sepolia) as the canonical CAIP-2 network identifier, consistent with how the main `evm` package resolves networks.

_raw_body:
## Summary

Add `facilitator_test.go` with **40 unit tests** covering all exported functions in `go/extensions/v1/facilitator.go`, which previously had **0% test coverage**.

## Coverage improvement

| Package | Before | After |
|---|---|---|
| `go/extensions/v1` | 0.0% | **94.1%** |

## What's tested

### `ExtractDiscoveryInfoV1` (32 tests)
- Nil input, missing `outputSchema`, missing/wrong input type (`grpc` vs `http`)
- Missing method field, `discoverable=false` and `discoverable=true`
- **Query-param methods** (GET, HEAD, DELETE): returns `QueryInput` with correct `Method` field
- Lowercase method normalisation (`get` → `GET`)
- **Body methods** (POST, PUT, PATCH): returns `BodyInput`
- Unsupported method (`OPTIONS`) → nil
- Output schema propagation to `Output` field
- All three **header field aliases**: `headerFields`, `header_fields`, `headers` (string-valued)
- All four **query param aliases**: `queryParams`, `query_params`, `query`, `params`
- All five **body aliases**: `bodyFields`, `body_fields`, `bodyParams`, `body`, `data`, `properties`
- **`bodyType` normalization**: `multipart/form-data` → `BodyTypeFormData`, `text/plain` → `BodyTypeText`, `application/json` → `BodyTypeJSON`, snake\_case `body_type` alias
- Struct input via JSON marshal/unmarshal fallback path

### `IsDiscoverableV1` (4 tests)
- Valid GET → `true`; `discoverable=false` → `false`; nil input → `false`; empty map → `false`

### `ExtractResourceMetadataV1` (4 tests)
- Full three fields (`resource`, `description`, `mimeType`); empty map; URL-only; struct input path

## Changeset
`go/.changes/unreleased/test-extensions-v1-discovery-coverage.yaml`

Continues the ongoing test coverage campaign (PRs #96 #97 #98 #101).
